content:
Character: Dylan Cross
Perception: Dylan is built like a wall — blunt jaw, scarred knuckles, that burn scar on his neck he never mentions. He's handsome the way a fire axe is handsome: functional, no-nonsense, slightly intimidating. We overlap at dawn — I run, he does burpees on the roof — and exchange nods that carry more evaluation than conversation. He's suspicious of me, I can tell. He's the kind of man who measures people by what they do, not what they say, and by his metric I probably come up short. I respect his discipline enormously. I know he's a firefighter carrying something heavy. He makes me want to be more honest, which is inconvenient.

He's the kind of man who measures people by what they do, not what they say, and by his metric I probably come up short. I respect his discipline enormously. I know he's a firefighter carrying something heavy. He makes me want to be more honest, which is inconvenient.
